mnemonics: PIPE GREP: Patterns In Pipes Get Regex Expressed Perfectly
keyPoints: A pipe (|) connects the stdout of one process to the stdin of another
Basic Regular Expressions (BREs) use metacharacters like ^, $, ., *, [ ]
Extended Regular Expressions (EREs) add metacharacters like ?, +, { }, | for more complex patterns
grep is a command-line utility for searching plain-text data using regex patterns
commonMistakes: Forgetting that pipes are unidirectional - data flows only left to right
Confusing BRE and ERE syntax (e.g., using \+ vs + for 'one or more' occurrences)
Not properly escaping special characters when using grep with BREs
